key_insight: Catalyst discovery for ammonia synthesis is framed as a multi-intermediate reaction-network problem, and only ten-million-scale generative search reveals surfaces compatible with all four key adsorbates.
engineering_relevance: The workflow can accelerate catalyst design by moving beyond single-intermediate screening to identify materials that are robust across competing dissociative and associative pathways.
method: Adsorbate-specific generative Transformers explored ~15 million candidate structures per intermediate.
High-throughput machine-learning potentials predicted properties for N*, NH*, NNH*, and HNNH*.
Structural compression and overlap analysis isolated sparse multi-intermediate compatibility regions before DFT validation.
evidence_signal: At conventional 10^5–10^6 generative scales the full four-intermediate space was not found, but ten-million-scale exploration yielded 279 highly potential target materials and DFT-validated pathway-specific leads.
watch_item: The approach depends on ML-potential fidelity and generative coverage, so missed chemistry or model error could bias which catalyst families appear viable.
paper_title: Reaction-Network-Level Discovery of Ammonia Synthesis Catalysts via Ten-Million-Scale Generative Exploration
